text.title: practices-django-coding Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for practices-django-coding. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Cross-app imports MUST use module-level aliases: `from apps.orders import models as order_models`. Own-module imports use relative: `from .models import User`. Never use `from apps.users.services import *`.
rule.rationale: Module-level aliases make renames O(1) — change the alias, not 40 call sites. Star imports shadow names silently and make agents lose track of provenance. Relative imports for own-module are idiomatic Python and shorter.

rule.statement: Services MUST be plain functions in `services/` modules, not classes. A class around a single method is a code smell. Use classes only when the service must hold connection or cache state across calls.
rule.rationale: Function-based services are simpler to test (no instantiation), simpler to type-check, and match the "Architecture Patterns with Python" (cosmicpython) approach. Agents default to god-service classes when left unconstrained.

rule.statement: Functions with more than two parameters MUST use multi-line parameter style with a bare `*` separator to enforce keyword-only arguments for optional params. Each parameter on its own line.
rule.rationale: Multi-line params make PR diffs readable (one param changed = one diff line), enable `ruff` to flag missing type hints per-param, and prevent positional-argument bugs in service calls.

rule.statement: Django/DRF views MUST be thin: validate input via serializer, call one service function, return serialized output. Views MUST NOT contain DB queries, business logic, or conditional branching beyond HTTP error cases. Target: under 20 lines per view method.
rule.rationale: Fat views are untestable without the HTTP layer. Thin views delegate to services which are testable as pure Python. Agents commonly re-create fat views when the service layer is not explicitly required in the prompt.

rule.statement: Every Django model MUST inherit from `BaseModel` which provides `uid` (UUIDField, unique=True), `created_at` (auto_now_add), and `updated_at` (auto_now). `BaseModel` is abstract. Never add these fields individually to each model.
rule.rationale: Missing standard fields is a recurring problem in new Django apps. UUID `uid` provides an external-safe identifier separate from the integer PK. Without a shared base, agents add or skip fields inconsistently across apps.

rule.statement: When placing a new function: DB mutations (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE) go in `services/`; external API calls go in `services/` or `integrations/`; pure functions (validation, calculation) go in `utils/`; data transformation goes in `utils/`.
rule.rationale: A predictable placement rule means agents and humans find code without grep. Without this rule agents default to putting everything in views or models.
